_meta.description: All historical and narrative text content for Wings Over No Man's Land. Extracted from index.html r2 and structured for editing by Luca (historical accuracy) and Vito (narrative voice) without touching game code. Review status workflow: draft → luca_approved → vito_approved → final. Entries marked final should not be edited without both Luca and Vito sign-off.

fields.name: German Invasion of Belgium and France
fields.intro_subtitle: The war has just begun. Welcome to Flanders, pilot.

belgium.author: Luca
belgium.notes: Intro subtitle displayed on game start screen for 4.2 seconds. Keep it terse and atmospheric — it overlays gameplay.

marne.notes: Campaign not yet implemented. Placeholder entry. Luca to provide historical briefing when campaign enters development.

ypres.notes: Campaign not yet implemented. Placeholder entry.

gallipoli.notes: Mediterranean Front. Campaign not yet implemented.

gorlice.notes: Eastern Front. Campaign not yet implemented.

fields.name: Nivelle Offensive / Second Battle of the Aisne

spring.notes: Campaign not yet implemented. Final campaign in the 10-campaign arc.

belgium_opening_patrol.author: Luca
belgium_opening_patrol.notes: First stage. Score range 0-35. No obstacles. Tone should suggest routine patrol, not combat.

belgium_flak_screen.author: Luca
belgium_flak_screen.notes: Second stage. Score range 35-75. Flak obstacles introduced.

belgium_observation_line.author: Luca
belgium_observation_line.notes: Third stage. Score range 75-105. Balloon-only stage. No enemy fighters.

belgium_contested_line.author: Luca
belgium_contested_line.notes: Fourth stage. Score range 105-145. Combined enemies and flak.

belgium_ace_pursuit.author: Luca
belgium_ace_pursuit.notes: Final endless stage. Score 145+. Enemy tier is one above player. High-stakes scoring zone.

fields.label: Fokker E.III
fields.hangar_note: One of Germany's first effective synchronized-gun fighters. Light, fast for its day, and a true early-war interrupter scout.

e3std.author: Luca
e3std.notes: Tier 1 Germany starter. Hangar_note is the description shown in the Hangar screen service record.

fields.label: Albatros D.V
fields.hangar_note: A sleek late-1917 German scout with elegant lines and strong speed, though less forgiving than the best late-war fighters.

albatrosdvstd.author: Luca
albatrosdvstd.notes: Tier 2 Germany. Unlocks after Belgium campaign.

fields.label: Fokker Dr.I
fields.hangar_note: Famous for its climb and agility, the Dr.I became an icon of the air war. It traded speed for maneuverability and close-quarters dogfighting.

dr1std.author: Luca
dr1std.notes: Tier 3 Germany. Unlocks after Ypres campaign.

fields.label: Fokker D.VII
fields.hangar_note: A superb late-war German fighter with strong climb, solid handling, and high-altitude performance. Many pilots considered it one of the war's best.

d7std.author: Luca
d7std.notes: Tier 4 Germany. Unlocks after Verdun campaign.

fields.label: Airco DH.1
fields.hangar_note: An early British pusher design built before the tractor fighter fully took over. Distinctive, experimental, and very much from the war's opening phase.

fields.label: Airco DH.2
fields.hangar_note: A nimble British pusher fighter that helped answer the early German monoplane threat. Light and agile, with good visibility for its pilot.

dh2.author: Luca
dh2.notes: Tier 2 Britain. Unlocks after Belgium campaign.

fields.label: Sopwith Triplane
fields.hangar_note: A light, elegant British triplane with impressive climb and nimble handling. It helped set the stage for later triplane dogfighting icons.

triplanestd.author: Luca
triplanestd.notes: Tier 3 Britain. Unlocks after Ypres campaign.

fields.label: Sopwith Pup
fields.hangar_note: Beloved by pilots for its pleasant handling and maneuverability. The Pup was smaller than later fighters, but highly effective in skilled hands.

pupstd.author: Luca
pupstd.notes: Tier 4 Britain. Unlocks after Gallipoli campaign.

fields.label: Sopwith Camel
fields.hangar_note: One of Britain's most famous fighters. Compact, powerful, and demanding to fly, the Camel became a deadly front-line dogfighter in skilled hands.

camelstd.author: Luca
camelstd.notes: Tier 5 Britain. Unlocks after Verdun campaign.

fields.label: Airco DH.9
fields.hangar_note: A late-war British day bomber with long range and a more purposeful strike profile. Bigger and steadier than the earlier scouts in the tree.

dh9.author: Luca
dh9.notes: Tier 6 Britain. Unlocks after Somme campaign.

fields.label: Red Baron
fields.note: The most famous German ace livery. Rich red coloring made the triplane instantly recognizable in the air.

dr1rb.author: Luca
dr1rb.notes: Based on Manfred von Richthofen's famous all-red Dr.I. Luca: verify the 'rich red' characterisation is consistent with historical record.

fields.label: Werner Voss
fields.note: A special Dr.I livery inspired by one of Germany's great fighter aces and the triplane's early legend.

dr1wevo.author: Luca
dr1wevo.notes: Werner Voss flew a Dr.I with distinctive facial markings. Luca: the note is vague — consider whether to name him explicitly.

fields.label: Josef Jacobs
fields.note: A late-war ace-themed triplane skin built around bold personal colors and squadron identity.

dr1jj.author: Luca
dr1jj.notes: Josef Jacobs was the highest-scoring surviving German ace. Consider adding kill count context if Vito approves.

fields.label: D.VII Ace Red
fields.note: A premium D.VII finish for players who want a more heroic late-war fighter profile.

d7rb.author: Luca
d7rb.notes: Currently uses generic copy. Vito: opportunity to add more flavour here.

fields.label: Field Green
fields.note: An alternate early-war monoplane finish suited to rough front-line reconnaissance and patrol duty.

fields.label: Black Wing
fields.note: A darker custom Albatros finish with a more ominous front-line silhouette.

fields.label: Diamond Banner
fields.note: A bold Albatros skin with a heraldic mid-fuselage pattern and rich unit-color flavor.

fields.label: Red Nose
fields.note: A bright personalized Albatros livery built around a red nose and more flamboyant late-war styling.

fields.label: Skull Flight
fields.note: A premium Albatros look with stark insignia and a theatrical squadron identity.

fields.label: Dun Tones
fields.note: A stylized Sopwith Pup livery with subdued tones that feel more weathered and field-ready.

fields.label: Silver Owl
fields.note: A cleaner, brighter custom Pup finish designed more as a reward aircraft than a standard patrol machine.

fields.label: Golden Flight
fields.note: A bold premium Pup look for event rewards or supporter unlocks.

game_title.author: Vito
game_title.notes: Canonical game title. Do not alter.

fields.value:
Tap or press SPACE to fly
X = BURST, C = RAPID

gameplay_hint.author: Vito
gameplay_hint.notes: Shown at bottom of screen during gameplay. Mobile players see Tap; desktop players see SPACE. May need splitting into mobile/desktop variants.

fields.value: Tap a side, view campaigns, open settings, or open the hangar

main_menu_hint.author: Vito
main_menu_hint.notes: Shown at bottom of splash/main menu screen.

fields.value: Review the latest changes, then continue to the main menu

tester_notes_hint.author: Vito
tester_notes_hint.notes: Shown on the testerNotes screen. Will be removed or repurposed post-beta.

xp_gained_hint.author: Vito
xp_gained_hint.notes: Shown in hint area when player accumulates enough kills for an XP point.

burst_engaged_hint.author: Vito
burst_engaged_hint.notes: Shown in hint area when Double Burst mode activates.

rapid_fire_engaged_hint.author: Vito
rapid_fire_engaged_hint.notes: Shown in hint area when Rapid Fire mode activates.

no_aces_recorded.author: Vito
no_aces_recorded.notes: Shown in leaderboard display area before any scores exist.

campaign_map_labels._note: Labels and subtitles for each node on the campaign map. These appear when a node is selected on the map screen.

belgium.label: Belgium
belgium.subtitle: German invasion of Belgium and France

aisne.label: Aisne
aisne.subtitle: Nivelle Offensive / Second Battle of the Aisne
